data.know.coziness.desc: The art of a home that feels like one. Comfort +6.
data.know.coziness.name: Coziness
data.know.effHeating.desc: More warmth from less fuel. Heating fuel use −25%.
data.know.effHeating.name: Efficient Heating
data.know.forecasting.desc: Reading the sky ahead. Cold-snap and avalanche forecast +1 day.
data.know.forecasting.name: Forecasting
data.know.gardening.desc: Growing anywhere. A small garden yields food +1/day, harvests +1.
data.know.gardening.name: Gardening
data.know.handiness.desc: Making do with less. Crafting materials −20%.
data.know.handiness.name: Handiness
data.know.hearthCraft.desc: Building a hearth in any home. Winter warmth and cold defense.
data.know.hearthCraft.name: Hearthcraft
data.know.insulation.desc: Sealing walls and windows against the cold. Every shelter gains insulation.
data.know.insulation.name: Insulation
data.know.preserve.desc: Keeping food from spoiling. Spoilage −50%, salt-cure +1.
data.know.preserve.name: Preservation
data.know.purify.desc: Gathering and filtering dew and rain. Clean water +1 daily.
data.know.purify.name: Purification
data.know.radioKnow.desc: Reaching the world by radio. Broadcast reach +1, weather forecast.
data.know.radioKnow.name: Radiocraft
data.know.scouting.desc: An eye for the way. Expedition success +4%p everywhere.
data.know.scouting.name: Scouting
data.know.tidiness.desc: A hand that keeps a home in order. Daily grime −0.5.
data.know.tidiness.name: Tidiness
data.knowbr.home.name: Homemaking
data.knowbr.lore.name: Knowing
data.knowbr.supply.name: Self-sufficiency
data.knowbr.warmth.name: Warmth
data.memo.com1.desc:
One per customer. Cutting the line means removal.
The sign stayed. The shelves were bones in three days.
data.memo.com1.name: Empty Shelf Sign
data.memo.com2.desc:
To my staff. We close today. Take what’s left, split it fairly.
I’m sorry I couldn’t keep you safe.
data.memo.com2.name: Manager’s Memo
data.memo.com3.desc:
Sprayed across shattered glass: "Already cleaned out. Don’t bother."
Below it someone added: "Checked anyway."
data.memo.com3.name: Graffiti on Broken Glass
data.memo.com4.desc:
Marker on cardboard: No cash. Trade only — water, meds, fuel.
It took a week for money to become paper.
data.memo.com4.name: No Cash Accepted
data.memo.com5.desc:
Need meds? Don’t knock — slip a list under the door. If we have it, it’s yours.
Last line: We have nothing left now.
data.memo.com5.name: Plea on the Pharmacy Shutter
data.memo.com6.desc:
Tonight, at the ration depot. We wait our turn no longer.
The flyer is pulped with rain. The depot burned that night.
data.memo.com6.name: Riot Night Flyer
data.memo.com7.desc:
Transaction cannot be completed. Please try again later.
The same line, lit for months now.
data.memo.com7.name: ATM Screen
data.memo.com8.desc:
Dear customers, we are closing. Calmly proceed to the nearest exit.
Handwritten in the margin: "Exit 3 blocked. No control."
data.memo.com8.name: Department Store Script
data.memo.ind1.desc:
By government order, all operations cease. Power down and go home at once.
Since the stamp on this page, the line has not moved.
data.memo.ind1.name: Plant Shutdown Order
data.memo.ind2.desc:
Night shift: 3 in. No day-shift handover — no one came.
Last line: Locking up. Leaving a light on.
data.memo.ind2.name: Last Shift Log
data.memo.ind3.desc:
Name and badge number inside a dusty hard hat. Below, small: "27 years. Going home now."
Twelve more still hang on the pegs.
data.memo.ind3.name: Name on a Hard Hat
data.memo.ind4.desc:
This month’s wages cannot be paid. So long as the company stands, you will be made whole.
The company did not stand.
data.memo.ind4.name: Unpaid Wages Notice
data.memo.ind5.desc:
Chalked on a pipe by the fitter: "Valves shut. I kept this heat going till the end."
The warmth went cold long ago.
data.memo.ind5.name: Boiler Room Scrawl
data.memo.ind6.desc:
The time cards all stop on one date. None punched after.
The clock still points to midnight.
data.memo.ind6.name: Stack of Time Cards
data.memo.ind7.desc:
Fuel drums 40 → 6. Parts crates all removed.
Bottom of the sheet: "Took everything we could carry. Sorry."
data.memo.ind7.name: Warehouse Inventory
data.memo.nw1.desc:
A file binder from beneath the collapsed relay station. Each surface signal has a number, coordinates, days endured.
My eyes stopped long on one line. I know those coordinates — my winters are written there.
data.memo.nw1.name: Ledger of Observed Subjects
data.memo.nw2.desc:
A typed memorandum. Those who endured alone. Those who kept records. Those who tended a fire — the criteria of "worth taking," set down item by item.
In the margin, a single stamp: "Selected."
data.memo.nw2.name: Memorandum of Selection
data.memo.nw3.desc:
A bound brief. A plan to gather the remaining survivors in one place and begin the "next humanity" — leadership, rules, even seating already decided.
Not a world we would build together. A world they had already drawn.
data.memo.nw3.name: Assembly Plan Brief
data.memo.nw4.desc:
A single sheet, left without an envelope. Coordinates, a date, and one short sentence.
"We have watched you for a long time. Your place is kept." — An invitation, or a summons.
data.memo.nw4.name: The Invitation
data.memo.obs1.name: Reception Note
data.memo.obs1.desc:
Roof antenna, three degrees south. Only comes in before dawn. Pictures taken from above — no idea who sends them, and no way to ask.
I tune in anyway. To see if our block is still there.
data.memo.obs2.name: Observation Log — Unsigned
data.memo.obs2.desc:
D+214. Grid 3, one light holding. Added to the list.
There is no signature.
data.memo.res1.desc:
Buy milk. Kids’ tuition. Mom’s birthday next week.
The list is still here. The store has been shut ten days.
data.memo.res1.name: Fridge Note
data.memo.res2.desc:
A child’s sneakers, still by the door. Too small — we meant to buy new ones.
We never did.
data.memo.res2.name: Shoes at the Door
data.memo.res3.desc:
First day of lockdown. Everyone said a few days, that’s all.
Waved to a neighbor across the way. That was the last hello.
data.memo.res3.name: Lockdown Day One
data.memo.res4.desc:
To all residents. The elevator is stopped. Water runs two hours a day.
The office closes today. Please, take care of yourselves.
data.memo.res4.name: Building Announcement
data.memo.res5.desc:
Pencil height marks on the door frame, close together — until last spring.
Nothing above them.
data.memo.res5.name: Height Marks
data.memo.res6.desc:
A few clothes, a photo album, passports. A bag packed halfway, then abandoned.
Where they meant to go isn’t written anywhere.
data.memo.res6.name: Half-Packed Suitcase
data.memo.res7.desc:
Going ahead. I’ll send word once I find us a spot at the supply line.
Still on the table. No reply ever came.
data.memo.res7.name: Letter on the Table
data.memo.res8.desc:
10kg rice, two cases of water, canned goods. Delivered.
After this date, no one left this house.
data.memo.res8.name: Last Delivery Slip
data.memo.rsc1.desc:
A logbook from the steel cabinet by the barrier. First page: isolate suspected cases, seal all passage. The last page is days later.
"We are shut in too. They locked the door from outside."
data.memo.rsc1.name: Checkpoint Control Log
data.memo.rsc10.desc:
A page transcribed from a terminal. Logs of regular contact with the orbital station. Most read "no surface signal."
Only the bottom line differs: "One signal detected. Coordinates logged. — Keep watching."
data.memo.rsc10.name: Satellite Uplink Log
data.memo.rsc11.desc:
A folded blueprint. Plans for a ground station that beams a signal back up to the orbital post. Antenna, transmitter, power — each drawn apart.
In the margin, by hand: "If someone raises this again, there will be an answer from above."
data.memo.rsc11.name: Radio Base Schematic
data.memo.rsc12.desc:
The notebook’s last page. "Nine winters, and the air will settle. I won’t see that far.
But if even one light lasts that long — please, switch this station back on. It is all I can leave. — Dr. ___"
data.memo.rsc12.name: The Last Entry
data.memo.rsc2.desc:
A laminated notice on the wall. Do not cross this line without a suit. Exposure cannot be undone.
Beneath it, in marker: "Even so, someone has to cross."
data.memo.rsc2.name: Protective Protocol Notice
data.memo.rsc3.desc:
A torn report page. Spread outran the models. Lockdown, it concludes, came too late.
In the margin, faint: "I no longer know if the cordon was to save people, or to hold them in."
data.memo.rsc3.name: Early Epidemiology Fragment
data.memo.rsc4.desc:
A map filling one wall. Cities ringed in concentric circles, the innermost marked thick in red.
I stared a long time at the one word in the legend: "Incineration."
data.memo.rsc4.name: The Cordon Map
data.memo.rsc5.desc:
Meeting minutes. A majority agreed there was only one way to stop the spread. The names of the three who dissented are struck out.
Last line: "Even if it brings winter early. So that those who remain might endure."
data.memo.rsc5.name: Minutes of the Decision
data.memo.rsc6.desc:
A folded page in a thick annex. It predicts that soot from mass incineration would veil the sun, and winter would last years.
"A winter mankind called down upon itself. We knew, and we pressed it anyway."
data.memo.rsc6.name: Climate Forecast Annex
data.memo.rsc7.desc:
The last entries from the undercroft lab’s access terminal. Most have no clock-out. One person keeps coming and going for days more.
After the ID, a title: Head, Observation Program. Where the name should be, a single initial.
data.memo.rsc7.name: Lab Access Log
data.memo.rsc8.desc:
A stamped cover sheet. A plan to track and log surviving signals on the ground by satellite, after the burning.
"We cannot come down. So we watch from above. As long as a light holds out, this is not a failure."
data.memo.rsc8.name: Observation Program Brief
data.memo.rsc9.desc:
A private note tucked among the research pads. "I was one of the three who signed against this.
So I chose to stay here, and watch to the end the winter I could not stop."
data.memo.rsc9.name: The Doctor’s Personal Note
data.memo.rst1.desc:
The front-desk screen still glows. Last booking: two nights, a suite, for two.
The check-out field is blank. They never went back down.
data.memo.rst1.name: The Last Booking
data.memo.rst2.desc:
A dozen pairs of skis still stand in the rack, some with name tags.
Who last rode the slope, there’s no way to know now.
data.memo.rst2.name: Skis Under the Snow
data.memo.rst3.desc:
Greetings in the guestbook by the hearth. "We’ll be back." "An unforgettable winter."
The last page, another hand: "Snowed in. Still — it’s warm here."
data.memo.rst3.name: The Lounge Guestbook
data.memo.rst4.desc:
A notice at the platform: Cable car suspended due to heavy snow. Service resumes when restored.
Restoration never came. The mountain kept its people as they were.
data.memo.rst4.name: Cable Car Suspended
data.memo.rst5.desc:
A small tree stands by the window. The bulbs are dark, the ornaments intact.
One gift, still unopened, waits beneath it.
data.memo.rst5.name: A Room’s Christmas Tree
data.memo.rst6.desc:
Morning class of five, afternoon cancelled. Too much snow.
Last line: Gathered the guests in the lounge. The way down is closed. Don’t frighten them.
data.memo.rst6.name: The Ski Instructor’s Notebook
data.memo.rst7.desc:
Folded towels still stack beside the open-air bath. The steam lifted long ago.
Someone, maybe, watched the world end from the warm water here.
data.memo.rst7.name: Towel Basket by the Spring
data.memo.rst8.desc:
A coin-op telescope points down the valley, the last coin still lodged in it.
What did they hope to see? There are no lights in the city below now.
data.memo.rst8.name: The Overlook Telescope
data.memo.shp1.desc:
A note nailed to the locked steel door. "The lower decks are flooded. The pump can’t keep up. I’ve locked the door."
The shanty on the deck must have been theirs. Honestly, it’s snugger out here.
data.memo.shp1.name: Note on the Cabin Door
data.memo.slum1.desc:
Checkmarks beside names. The pen stops halfway. Below, only ruled lines.
Those not on the list got nothing.
data.memo.slum1.name: Ration List
data.memo.slum2.desc:
"We were not on the list."
The paint ran and set that way.
data.memo.slum2.name: Scrawl on the Plank Wall
data.memo.slum3.desc:
A ration coupon printed to look real. On the back: "Ten of these for a jug of water. Worth a shot."
In the end they were good nowhere.
data.memo.slum3.name: Forged Ration Coupon
data.memo.slum4.desc:
A house and four people in crayon, painted over all in grey.
In one corner, uneven letters: "Our home."
data.memo.slum4.name: A Child’s Drawing
data.memo.slum5.desc:
This zone is excluded from assistance. Relocate by your own means.
It never said where to go.
data.memo.slum5.name: Evacuation Notice
data.memo.slum6.desc:
One jug per household a day. Keep the order. No fighting.
In a different hand at the bottom: "Well’s dry. Sorry."
data.memo.slum6.name: Rules of the Shared Well
data.memo.slum7.desc:
A folded blanket and an empty bowl at the alley’s end. Someone sat here a long while.
Where they rose and went, no one knows.
data.memo.slum7.name: The Left-Behind Blanket
data.memo.stair1.desc: Where does this passage lead? The boot prints go only downward.
data.memo.stair1.name: Scrawl on the Landing
data.memo.sub1.desc:
All train service has stopped. Do not wait on the platform — proceed to a surface shelter.
The same message looped, then cut off mid-sentence.
data.memo.sub1.name: Platform Announcement
data.memo.sub2.desc:
Chalk numbers queued before the gate shutter, past three hundred.
By the last number, small: "This far. Inside is full."
data.memo.sub2.name: The Line at the Shutter
data.memo.sub3.desc:
An empty pram lies on its side in the track gravel. The blanket is still folded.
Why it was left here, no one wrote down.
data.memo.sub3.name: A Pram on the Tracks
data.memo.sub4.desc:
On the wall timetable, one line is circled hard in red pen: last train, 23:40.
Beneath it: "Miss this and walk down."
data.memo.sub4.name: Last Train Timetable
data.memo.sub5.desc:
At the dusty platform’s end, footprints file into the dark of the tunnel. None come back.
What they hoped to find underground — I think I’m beginning to understand.
data.memo.sub5.name: Footprints into the Tunnel

data.region.customsyard.desc: A graveyard of cargo that never cleared customs · anything could surface
data.region.customsyard.name: Customs Impound
data.region.deptstore.desc: A palace of luxury beneath the towers · peak cloth and fine furniture

data.region.harborYard.desc: Container cargo · what the sea gave today

data.shelter.bridgehouse.desc: A stone keeper house beside the fallen span. By day the broken bridge hangs in the window; by night, every star in the sky.
data.shelter.bridgehouse.limits: A house over the gorge — the winter wind is loud
data.shelter.bridgehouse.name: Bridge Keeper House
data.shelter.bridgehouse.perk.label: A seat under the stars — the night sky, unbroken
data.shelter.bunker.desc: A half-collapsed dome bunker. Stars peek through the cracked shell, but inside the thick walls it is surprisingly snug.
data.shelter.bunker.limits: Sealed structure — losing power halts shelter bonuses & traits
data.shelter.bunker.name: Dome Bunker
data.shelter.bunker.perk.label: Thick shell — injuries heal twice as fast
data.shelter.bunker.upkeep.label: Battery 1 / day (ventilation)
data.shelter.bus.desc: A school bus stalled on the highway. Cramped, but it feels like it could take you anywhere.
data.shelter.bus.name: Abandoned School Bus
data.shelter.bus.perk.label: Mobile base — expedition time -25%
data.shelter.bus.upkeep.label: Fuel 1 / 2 days
data.shelter.cabin.desc: A cabin on the forest’s edge. The coziest refuge you have found in this ruined world.
data.shelter.cabin.limits: Timber roof — bad weather needs 1 material/day for leak repair (else cleanliness -8)
data.shelter.cabin.name: Forest Cabin
data.shelter.cabin.perk.label: Cozy layout — comfort effects ×1.5
data.shelter.cabin.upkeep.label: Building material 1 / 3 days
data.shelter.container.desc: A cargo container abandoned in the middle of the wasteland. Cramped, but it keeps out the wind and rain.
data.shelter.container.limits: Thin steel — comfort -8 on rainy/snowy days
data.shelter.container.name: Abandoned Container
data.shelter.container.perk.label: Crossroads outpost — expedition success +5%p
data.shelter.controltower.desc: The top of a control tower over the harbor. Glass on all sides makes the wind loud, but the coming weather shows here first.
data.shelter.controltower.limits: Glass on all sides — comfort -6 on rainy/snowy days
data.shelter.controltower.name: Harbor Control Tower
data.shelter.controltower.perk.label: High vantage — weather forecast· cold-snap lead +1 day· success +2%p
data.shelter.controltower.upkeep.label: Battery 1 / day (control console)
data.shelter.customs.desc: The inspection hall of a border checkpoint. Luggage that never cleared still sits on the belt — now the border stands open for one.
data.shelter.customs.limits: Wind slips through the booths — comfort -5 in bad weather (fix by sealing them)
data.shelter.customs.name: Customs House
data.shelter.customs.perk.label: Home at the gate — threshold of the eastern metropolis
data.shelter.greenhouse.desc: A glass greenhouse that survived as if by miracle. Even at the end of the world, seeds still sprout from the soil.
data.shelter.greenhouse.limits: Glass roof — snowy days need 1 material to patch (else cleanliness -8)
data.shelter.greenhouse.name: Greenhouse
data.shelter.greenhouse.perk.label: Garden — food +1 daily
data.shelter.greenhouse.upkeep.label: Clean water 1 / day (irrigation)
data.shelter.lighthouse.desc: The top floor of a lighthouse at the cliff’s edge. Snug within thick walls, with a rain catch beside the rooftop lantern.
data.shelter.lighthouse.limits: Rooftop rain catch — clean water +2 on rainy/snowy days (self-sufficient)
data.shelter.lighthouse.name: Lighthouse Lamp Room
data.shelter.lighthouse.perk.label: Searchlight — success +3%p in all regions· weather forecast
data.shelter.lighthouse.upkeep.label: Fuel 1 / 2 days (beacon)
data.shelter.lodge.desc: A timber lodge at the highland resort. Outside is the harshest winter in the world; before the hearth, it is warmer than anywhere.
data.shelter.lodge.limits: Altitude — fuel use +30%· cold snaps more frequent (offset by lodge insulation & hearth)
data.shelter.lodge.name: Ski Lodge
data.shelter.lodge.perk.label: Hearth lodge — comfort effects ×1.3· weather forecast
data.shelter.lodge.upkeep.label: Fuel 1 / day (hearth & altitude heating)
data.shelter.penthouse.desc: A penthouse at cloud height. The towers stand around you like castle spires, and below, a city drowned in fog.
data.shelter.penthouse.limits: High-rise wind — winter rattles the glass
data.shelter.penthouse.name: Penthouse
data.shelter.penthouse.perk.label: A throne among spires — the whole city hangs in the window
data.shelter.rooftop.desc: Atop a fallen city building. A crude room walled with scavenged panels sits on the concrete slab, beside a yard you can turn into a garden.
data.shelter.rooftop.limits: Two slates are missing from the roof — until repaired, cleanliness dips a little on rainy/snowy days
data.shelter.rooftop.name: City Rooftop
data.shelter.rooftop.perk.label: Clear vantage — salvage 1 furniture on partial success· rooftop garden yields ×2
data.shelter.ship.desc: The deck of a passenger liner run aground on the coast. You sleep to the sound of waves and cast a line at dawn.
data.shelter.ship.limits: Sea damp — cleanliness drops 2 faster each day
data.shelter.ship.name: Liner Cabin
data.shelter.ship.perk.label: Fishing — food +1 daily· salvage some resources even on failed expeditions
data.shelter.ship.perk.produceNote: Caught a fish with night fishing
data.shelter.ship.upkeep.label: Parts 1 / 3 days (bilge pump)
data.shelter.subway.desc: A platform sleeping beneath the fallen city. Untouched by weather or season — master the dark and it becomes the finest fortress.
data.shelter.subway.limits: Total darkness — comfort -12 if no light is lit
data.shelter.subway.name: Subway Station
data.shelter.subway.perk.label: Rest in the dark — lighting comfort effect ×1.5
data.shelter.subway.upkeep.label: Battery 1 / day (ventilation fan)
data.shelter.terminal.desc: One corner beneath the great arched ceiling, beside the ticket booths. Sun falls through the broken roof, and in that pool of light a tree grows.
data.shelter.terminal.limits: Broken ceiling — comfort -4 in bad weather (fix by patching the gap)
data.shelter.terminal.name: Grand Terminal Hall
data.shelter.terminal.perk.label: A hall with a pool of light — a sanctuary mid-city
data.shelter.tugboat.desc: An old yacht moored at the pier. The saloon is snug but warm, and on the water the line never runs dry.
data.shelter.tugboat.limits: Deck damp — cleanliness drops 2 faster each day
data.shelter.tugboat.name: Yacht
data.shelter.tugboat.perk.label: Home on the water — food +1 daily (ice fishing available)
data.shelter.tugboat.perk.produceNote: Caught a fish off the gunwale
data.shelter.tugboat.upkeep.label: Fuel 1 / 2 days (engine warmup)
data.sketch.aurora.desc:
A green curtain drifted slow above the ridgeline. There was no sound.
That something so quiet could be so vast — tonight, that was a comfort.
data.sketch.aurora.name: Aurora Sketch
data.sketch.comet.desc:
It passed low to the west, dragging its tail. When it swings back, I won’t be here.
Still — I write it down: tonight, at least one person saw it.
data.sketch.comet.name: Comet Sketch
data.sketch.meteor.desc:
I lost count of how many. I stopped counting and just stood a long while, watching.
Even the falling ones must have wanted someone to wish on them.
data.sketch.meteor.name: Meteor Shower Sketch
data.sketch.milkyway.desc:
When the city still lived I never saw a sky like this. Of what the ruin gave, this one isn’t bad.
Beyond the dust, stars flowing like a river.
data.sketch.milkyway.name: Milky Way Sketch
data.sketch.moonhalo.desc:
A faint ring hung round the moon. Someone once said it means snow tomorrow.
The sky is the only forecast left now.
data.sketch.moonhalo.name: Moon Halo Sketch
data.sketch.satellite.desc:
A single point slid between the stars at a steady pace. It did not blink, and it did not fall.
That is no star. Someone up there is still going round.
data.sketch.satellite.name: Orbiting Light Sketch

data.theme.greencorner.name: Green Corner

data.theme.workshop.name: Work Space

data.weather.snow.name: Snow
data.weather.storm.name: Downpour
data.will.will1.desc:
No strength left to wait. When spring comes to that window, may someone use this room.
Don’t hate me. I held on a long time.
data.will.will1.name: Note on the Sill
data.will.will2.desc:
Son, the fuel is hid in the attic. Ration it to spring and you’ll live.
I won’t eat your share. Please — live.
data.will.will2.name: Father’s Last Words
data.will.will3.desc:
We chose to go together. Better than being left apart.
Whoever finds this house — may you not be alone.
data.will.will3.name: Letter for Two
data.will.will4.desc:
I stayed to the last patient. The medicine ran out long ago; all I had left was a held hand.
Now it’s my turn. I’d be lying if I said I wasn’t afraid.
data.will.will4.name: The Nurse’s Notebook
data.will.will5.desc:
I left the door open. Outlive me.
Whoever meets this one — he’s a good dog. Just easily frightened.
data.will.will5.name: A Word for the Dog
data.will.will6.desc:
I got your letter. I wanted to say I missed you too — I needed to say it.
There’s nowhere left to send this now.
data.will.will6.name: The Reply Never Sent
